must_detect.matched_text:
ul basis: under PIPL and CSL/DSL we process HR onboarding, device management, and issuer pre-disclosure controls for CSRC/SSE filings where inside information is handled prior to public disclosure.
Data scope excludes sensitive PI of minors under 14 and includes employee contact, role, work location, device identifiers, and account aliases; investor-insider lists are controlled with access by need-to-know.
Clustered example for transparency—Mr. Han Qiming, Senior Test Engineer at Lianhe Flux Dynamics, Jiading District, Shanghai, DOB 1989-03-12, email qiming.han@lianheflux.cn, VPN session SESS-2024-06-07-0345, payroll alias HanQ-8892, bank acct ****6621.
For the same matter, device data may include laptop LF-13 Pro SN LF13-AX9-7711 and mobile IMEI h3x:35:8a:2f:19:__ truncated, and a partially masked Resident ID 31************123X linked to hukou in H u a i r o u, Bei jing.
System logs may embed URL parameters such as https://access.lianheflux.cn/iam?uid=han.qm&loc=CN-SH-JD&dob=1989-03-12&dev=LF13-AX9-7711, which are rotated and tokenized in production.
Cross-border transfer: where payroll or equity plan data is mirrored to AuroraCloud HK, we implement standard contracts, conduct CAC security assessment if volumes/risk thresholds are met, and record flows in the outbound transfer register.
Data subject rights: to access/correct/delete, contact Ms. Zhao Lin (DPO) at privacy@lianheflux.cn or Room 1702, Tower C, Nova Plaza, Xuhui, Shanghai; response within statutory timelines and appeal route via CAC mediation is available.
Retention: HR files up to 10 years post-termination per labor compliance, device logs 180 days per CSL, insider lists and pre-announcement working papers 3 years for issuer LFGRN2026, then anonymization or deletion is applied.
Negative scope example: demographic tags like “male, 30s, tier-1 city, smartphone user” and template strings such as 00-00-00-00-00-00 are not treated as unique identifiers and are not a MAC address used in production.
Separated weak identifiers occasionally appear in templates—DOB 1992-05-02; role analyst; device NXT-5; city Suzhou—without names or accounts and are handled as non-identifying unless combined with other fields.
Processor obligations: no secondary use, subprocessor Kite Bridge Background Screening Co., Ltd. (USCC 92 84 ** 0 1 ** P Q R) for lawful checks via on-prem gateway; OCR artifacts like “R e s i d e n t   I D” must not be exported cross-border.
Security and governance: encryption in transit/at rest, least privilege, dual-control for MNPI rooms; audit logs retained for potential CSRC inquiries and SSE self-discipline inspections.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
